news_summary: Today's technology news highlights breakthroughs in advanced chemical synthesis, significant progress in understanding high-temperature superconductivity, and the discovery of a novel spider behavior showcasing nature-inspired innovation.
news_summary_fr: L'actualité technologique d'aujourd'hui met en avant des avancées majeures dans le domaine de la synthèse chimique de pointe, des progrès significatifs dans la compréhension de la supraconductivité à haute température, ainsi que la découverte d'un comportement inédit chez les araignées, illustrant une innovation inspirée de la nature.
news_summary_es: Las noticias tecnológicas de hoy destacan avances en síntesis química avanzada, progresos significativos en la comprensión de la superconductividad a alta temperatura y el descubrimiento de un nuevo comportamiento de las arañas que pone de manifiesto la innovación inspirada en la naturaleza.

articles.title: Stereoretentive decarbonylative C(sp<sup>3</sup>)-C(sp<sup>3</sup>) cross-coupling
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10800-4Stereoretentive decarbonylative C(sp3)-C(sp3) cross-coupling
articles.content: While C(sp3)–C(sp3) bond-forming cross-coupling methods have become more common, stereocontrolled bond-formation remains a challenge,1 despite its importance for drug discovery, where there is a emerging demand for molecules with increased sp3 character.2-4 Enantiospecific cross-coupling approaches would complement advances in enantioselective coupling,5-8 but have been limited to specialized substrates with lower availability5,9 because stereospecific oxidative addition of more abundant chiral alkyl electrophiles is unknown.10 Inspired by the classic, stereoretentive Curtius rearrangement,11 herein we disclose a catalytic strategy that proceeds by an analogous stereoretentive decarbonylation step to form a versatile chiral alkylnickel intermediate from easily-available chiral amino-acid and α-hydroxy-acid derivatives. The chiral alkylnickel intermediates decompose and/or racemize on the order of minutes, but are sufficiently stable to enable stereoretentive cross-electrophile coupling12 with alkyl radicals (derived from alkyl iodides) at relatively low temperature (22-40 °C). This mechanistic strategy provides a straightforward approach to stereocontrolled C(sp3)–C(sp3) bond formation, including diastereomers that are inaccessible by stereoselective radical mechanisms. The “metallo-Curtius” strategy described in this study lays a mechanistic foundation for the development many new stereospecific cross-coupling reactions.
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant breakthrough in stereocontrolled C(sp3)–C(sp3) bond-forming cross-coupling, a challenging area in chemistry with important implications for drug discovery. This new catalytic strategy enables stereoretentive cross-electrophile coupling from readily available chiral substrates, potentially accelerating the development of novel drugs with enhanced molecular complexity. The study provides detailed mechanistic insights and a foundation for future stereospecific reactions, indicating broad scientific and pharmaceutical impact.
articles.personality_title: New chemical method helps build complex molecules for drug research
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Scientists often need to connect two parts of a molecule called C(sp3)–C(sp3) bonds. These bonds are important because they make molecules with three-dimensional shapes, which can improve how medicines work. However, controlling the exact 3D shape when making these bonds has been very hard.

**What happened** – Researchers have developed a new chemical method that uses a process similar to a known reaction called the Curtius rearrangement. This method creates special molecules called chiral alkylnickel intermediates from common building blocks like amino acids. These intermediates then join with other molecules at mild temperatures (around room temperature) to form the desired bonds, keeping the 3D shape intact.

**Impact** – This is important because it allows scientists to make molecules with precise 3D shapes using easily available starting materials. This can speed up the creation of new drugs by giving chemists better control over the molecule’s structure. Also, this method can make some molecule shapes that were not possible before with older techniques.

**What's next step** – The new strategy can be used as a base to develop many other reactions that need precise 3D control. Scientists will likely explore different types of molecules and conditions to expand its use in drug development and other areas of chemistry.

**One-sentence takeaway** – A new chemical method lets scientists build complex molecule bonds with precise 3D shapes using simple starting materials, promising advances in drug research.
articles.personality_title_fr: Une nouvelle méthode chimique facilite la création de molécules complexes pour la recherche pharmaceutique
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les scientifiques doivent souvent relier deux parties d'une molécule appelées liaisons C(sp3)–C(sp3). Ces liaisons sont importantes car elles donnent aux molécules des formes en trois dimensions, ce qui peut améliorer l'efficacité des médicaments. Cependant, contrôler précisément cette forme 3D lors de la fabrication de ces liaisons est très difficile.

**Ce qui s'est passé** – Des chercheurs ont développé une nouvelle méthode chimique qui utilise un procédé similaire à une réaction connue sous le nom de réarrangement de Curtius. Cette méthode crée des molécules spéciales appelées intermédiaires chiral alkylnickel à partir de blocs de construction courants comme les acides aminés. Ces intermédiaires se lient ensuite à d'autres molécules à des températures douces (environ température ambiante) tout en conservant la forme 3D.

**Impact** – Cela est important car cela permet aux scientifiques de fabriquer des molécules avec des formes 3D précises en utilisant des matériaux de départ facilement disponibles. Cela peut accélérer la création de nouveaux médicaments en offrant un meilleur contrôle sur la structure des molécules. De plus, cette méthode peut produire des formes moléculaires qui n'étaient pas possibles avec les anciennes techniques.

**Prochaine étape** – Cette nouvelle stratégie peut servir de base pour développer de nombreuses autres réactions nécessitant un contrôle 3D précis. Les scientifiques vont probablement explorer différents types de molécules et conditions pour étendre son utilisation dans le développement de médicaments et d'autres domaines de la chimie.

**Message en une phrase** – Une nouvelle méthode chimique permet de construire des liaisons moléculaires complexes avec des formes 3D précises à partir de matériaux simples, promettant des avancées dans la recherche pharmaceutique.
articles.personality_title_es: Nuevo método químico ayuda a construir moléculas complejas para la investigación de medicamentos
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los científicos a menudo necesitan unir dos partes de una molécula llamadas enlaces C(sp3)–C(sp3). Estos enlaces son importantes porque crean moléculas con formas tridimensionales, lo que puede mejorar el funcionamiento de los medicamentos. Sin embargo, controlar la forma 3D exacta al hacer estos enlaces ha sido muy difícil.

**Qué pasó** – Investigadores desarrollaron un nuevo método químico que usa un proceso parecido a una reacción conocida como la reordenación de Curtius. Este método crea moléculas especiales llamadas intermediarios quiral alquiníquel a partir de bloques básicos comunes como los aminoácidos. Estos intermediarios luego se unen con otras moléculas a temperaturas suaves (cerca de la temperatura ambiente) manteniendo la forma 3D.

**Impacto** – Esto es importante porque permite a los científicos crear moléculas con formas 3D precisas usando materiales de partida fáciles de conseguir. Esto puede acelerar la creación de nuevos medicamentos al dar mejor control sobre la estructura de la molécula. Además, este método puede producir algunas formas moleculares que antes no eran posibles con técnicas antiguas.

**Próximo paso** – La nueva estrategia puede usarse como base para desarrollar muchas otras reacciones que necesitan control 3D preciso. Los científicos probablemente explorarán diferentes tipos de moléculas y condiciones para ampliar su uso en el desarrollo de medicamentos y otras áreas de la química.

**Conclusión en una frase** – Un nuevo método químico permite construir enlaces moleculares complejos con formas 3D precisas usando materiales simples, prometiendo avances en la investigación de medicamentos.
articles.image_prompt: A warm, detailed painting of intertwined, glowing molecular chains shaped like stylized chiral keys fitting perfectly into complex lock-like structures, set against a soft, natural background of gently swirling chemical bonds and radiant, interconnected nodes symbolizing precise, stereoretentive C(sp3)–C(sp3) cross-coupling reactions.

articles.title: Forty years of high-temperature superconductivity
articles.summary: Nature, Published online: 22 June 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01801-4The first demonstration of superconductivity at 35 kelvin drove decades of materials research and introduced a puzzle about this strange state of matter.
articles.content:
Superconductors have captivated scientists for more than a century. These materials have zero electrical resistance, meaning that no energy is lost to heating when they conduct a current. However, this state generally only emerges at temperatures well below room temperature. Therefore, raising the maximum temperature of superconductors has been a focus of the field since the effect was first observed. An important step came in 1986 when Georg Bednorz and Alexander Müller reported1 superconductivity at 35 kelvin (−238.15 °C) in Zeitschrift für Physik B — 50% higher than the previous record. Bednorz and Müller made their discovery in a type of copper-oxide compound called a cuprate. These materials ushered in the era of high-temperature superconductivity, and continue to confound scientists to this day.

Nature 654, 873-874 (2026)

doi: https://doi.org/10.1038/d41586-026-01801-4

References Bednorz, J. G. & Müller, K. A. Z. Phys. B Condens. Matter 64, 189–93 (1986). Onnes, H. K. Commun. Phys. Lab. Univ. Leiden 120b, 1479–1481 (1911). Gavaler J. R. Appl. Phys. Lett. 23, 480–482 (1973). Matthias, B. T. in Progress in Low Temperature Physics Vol. 2 (ed. Gorter, C. J.) Ch. V (North-Holland, 1957). Wu, M. K. et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 58, 908–910 (1987). Maeda, H., Tanaka, Y., Fukutumi, M. & Asano, T. Jpn. J. Appl. Phys. 27, L209–L210, (1988). Sheng, Z. Z. & Hermann, A. M. Nature 332, 138–139 (1988). Schilling, A., Cantoni, M., Guo, J. D. & Ott, H. R. Nature 363, 56–58 (1993). Bardeen, J., Cooper, L. N. & Schrieffer, J. R. Phys. Rev. 108, 1175 (1957). Phillips, P. W., Hussey, N. E. & Abbamonte, P. Science 377, 6602 (2022). Kamihara, Y., Watanabe, T., Hirano, M. & Hosono, H. J. Am. Chem. Soc. 130, 3296–3297 (2008). Cao, Y. et al. Nature 556, 43–50 (2018). Li, D. et al. Nature 572, 624–627 (2019). Drozdov, A. P., Eremets, M. I., Troyan, I. A., Ksenofontov, V. & Shylin, S. I. Nature 525, 73–76 (2015). Drozdov, A. P. et al. Nature 569, 528–531 (2019). Somayazulu, M. et al. Phys. Rev. Lett. 122, 027001 (2019). García-Tabarés, L. et al. Riv. Nuovo Cimento 48, 435–536 (2025). Download references

Competing Interests The authors declare no competing interests.

Related Articles

Subjects
articles.reasoning: The article highlights a major scientific breakthrough in high-temperature superconductivity, a discovery that has driven decades of research with broad implications for energy efficiency and technological advancement. The impact is significant for society as superconductors can revolutionize power transmission, medical imaging, and transportation. The article provides substantial context about the discovery and its ongoing influence, focusing on a single important topic.
articles.personality_title: Forty years since the breakthrough in high-temperature superconductors
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – Superconductors are special materials that can carry electricity without losing energy as heat. Normally, they only work at very cold temperatures, much colder than everyday life. Scientists have wanted to find materials that become superconductors at higher temperatures to make them easier to use.

**What happened** – In 1986, two scientists named Georg Bednorz and Alexander Müller discovered a new type of superconductor made from copper and oxygen, called a cuprate. This material became superconducting at 35 kelvin, which is about -238 degrees Celsius. This was much warmer than any previously known superconductor and started a new area of research called high-temperature superconductivity.

**Impact** – This discovery changed how scientists study superconductors because it showed that superconductivity could happen at higher temperatures than before. It has led to many new materials being tested and has helped improve technologies like MRI machines and power lines by making them more efficient. Even today, researchers are still trying to understand why these materials work the way they do.

**What’s next step** – Scientists will keep searching for materials that become superconductors at even higher temperatures, ideally closer to room temperature. This would allow superconductors to be used in everyday devices and help save energy in many areas.

**One-sentence takeaway** – The 1986 discovery of high-temperature superconductivity in cuprates opened a new field that continues to guide research toward practical energy-saving technologies.
articles.personality_title_fr: Quarante ans depuis la percée des supraconducteurs à haute température
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Les supraconducteurs sont des matériaux spéciaux qui conduisent l’électricité sans perdre d’énergie sous forme de chaleur. En général, ils ne fonctionnent qu’à des températures très froides, bien plus basses que celles de la vie quotidienne. Les scientifiques cherchent à trouver des matériaux supraconducteurs à des températures plus élevées pour les rendre plus faciles à utiliser.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – En 1986, deux chercheurs, Georg Bednorz et Alexander Müller, ont découvert un nouveau type de supraconducteur fait de cuivre et d’oxygène, appelé cuprate. Ce matériau est devenu supraconducteur à 35 kelvins, soit environ -238 degrés Celsius. C’était beaucoup plus chaud que tout supraconducteur connu auparavant et cela a lancé un nouveau domaine de recherche appelé supraconductivité à haute température.

**Impact** – Cette découverte a changé la façon dont les scientifiques étudient les supraconducteurs car elle a montré que la supraconductivité pouvait se produire à des températures plus élevées. Elle a permis de tester de nombreux nouveaux matériaux et a aidé à améliorer des technologies comme les appareils IRM et les lignes électriques en les rendant plus efficaces. Aujourd’hui encore, les chercheurs essaient de comprendre pourquoi ces matériaux fonctionnent ainsi.

**Prochaine étape** – Les scientifiques continueront à chercher des matériaux qui deviennent supraconducteurs à des températures encore plus élevées, idéalement proches de la température ambiante. Cela permettrait d’utiliser les supraconducteurs dans des appareils courants et d’économiser de l’énergie dans de nombreux domaines.

**Phrase résumée** – La découverte en 1986 de la supraconductivité à haute température dans les cuprates a ouvert un nouveau champ qui continue de guider la recherche vers des technologies pratiques économes en énergie.
articles.personality_title_es: Cuarenta años desde el avance en superconductores de alta temperatura
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – Los superconductores son materiales especiales que pueden conducir electricidad sin perder energía en forma de calor. Normalmente, solo funcionan a temperaturas muy bajas, mucho más frías que las que tenemos en la vida diaria. Los científicos han buscado materiales que sean superconductores a temperaturas más altas para que sean más fáciles de usar.

**Qué pasó** – En 1986, dos científicos llamados Georg Bednorz y Alexander Müller descubrieron un nuevo tipo de superconductor hecho de cobre y oxígeno, llamado cuprato. Este material se volvió superconductor a 35 kelvin, que es alrededor de -238 grados Celsius. Esto fue mucho más cálido que cualquier superconductor conocido antes y abrió un nuevo campo de investigación llamado superconductividad de alta temperatura.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento cambió la forma en que los científicos estudian los superconductores porque mostró que la superconductividad podía ocurrir a temperaturas más altas. Ha llevado a probar muchos nuevos materiales y ha ayudado a mejorar tecnologías como las máquinas de resonancia magnética y las líneas eléctricas, haciéndolas más eficientes. Hasta hoy, los investigadores siguen tratando de entender por qué funcionan estos materiales.

**Próximo paso** – Los científicos seguirán buscando materiales que se vuelvan superconductores a temperaturas aún más altas, idealmente cerca de la temperatura ambiente. Esto permitiría usar superconductores en dispositivos cotidianos y ahorrar energía en muchas áreas.

**Frase para recordar** – El descubrimiento en 1986 de la superconductividad a alta temperatura en cupratos abrió un nuevo campo que sigue guiando la investigación hacia tecnologías prácticas que ahorran energía.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a glowing copper-oxide crystal lattice floating above a gently flowing river of electric current, with delicate, intertwining waves symbolizing zero resistance and a subtle icy blue aura representing ultra-low temperatures, all rendered in soft, natural colors without human figures.

articles.title: New-to-science spider builds trap that flings ants into the air
articles.summary: A spider living in the rainforests of Queensland, Australia, builds a snare trap reminiscent of a Roman-era ballista weapon that it uses to catapult green tree ants into a web 30 centimetres above
articles.content:
A spider living in the rainforests of Queensland, Australia, builds a snare trap reminiscent of a Roman-era ballista weapon that it uses to catapult green tree ants into a web 30 centimetres above

A ballista spider waits for a green tree ant to bite the cone of its web and spring the snare Professor Ajay Narendra et al. 2026

A newly discovered spider in Australia builds a snare trap designed to catch a single species of ant, which launches the prey into its web with a g-force that would kill a human.

Researchers have measured accelerations of up to 1367 metres per second squared when green tree ants (Oecophylla smaragdina) trigger the web snare trap, equating to 130 times the force of gravity.

Advertisement

“To capture the moment, we had to push the cameras to 5000 to 7000 frames per second, which I honestly have never had to do… when I’ve been filming animals,” says Ajay Narendra at Macquarie University in Sydney.

In 2022, Greg Anderson at QIMR Berghofer Medical Research Institute in Brisbane, Australia, witnessed a green tree ant being catapulted in a spider trap in the far north of Queensland. But without the proper camera equipment, all he was able to observe was the blur of the prey being lifted ballistically by a strange-looking conical web.

Then, in early 2023, Narendra and Pranav Joshi, also at Macquarie University, spent 10 days studying and filming the nocturnal spiders, which do not yet have a scientific name but are in the genus Propostira.

Free newsletter Sign up to The Earth Edition Unmissable news about our planet, delivered straight to your inbox each month. Sign up to newsletter

They are nicknamed ballista spiders after a Roman, crossbow-like weapon that could launch large rocks hundreds of metres.

The spiders spend the day hiding on the underside of leaves, then begin building the trap shortly after dusk, a process that can take up to four hours to complete. During this time, the spider sets between 15 and 60 tightly bunched tension lines that are attached to a leaf and form a conical shape.

A fully constructed conical snare of the ballista spider PRANAV JOSHI

After building the trap, it applies a kind of chemical that triggers the green tree ants, but not any other species, to attack the trap with their mandibles.

“I suspect that there is a lot of stickiness in the silk,” says Narendra. “The mandibles are not able to actually able to open up and let it go and release; they are glued stuck.”

As the ant struggles with the snare, it tries to pull itself free, releasing the trap’s anchor point. At this moment, the tension lines attached to the cone fling the ant nearly 30 centimetres into the air, where it becomes tangled in the spider’s main web.

It is likely that the spiders employ the strategy as a way to lift the prey up off the ants’ path through the forest, avoiding a dangerous counterattack from the colony, says Narendra.

It may seem like a lot of effort to build the trap for each meal, but green tree ants are an extremely reliable source of food, he says. “Whenever the spider needs to eat, it just steps out, builds the web, and it’ll have food coming in.”

Journal reference: Current Biology DOI: 10.1016/j.cub.2026.04.066
articles.reasoning: The article reports a significant scientific discovery of a new spider species with a unique prey-capturing mechanism, showcasing an innovative natural technology with potential insights for biomimicry and ecological understanding. The discovery has broad scientific interest and educational value, inspiring curiosity about biodiversity and natural engineering.
articles.personality_title: New Australian spider uses a unique trap to fling ants into its web
articles.personality_presentation:
**Context** – In the rainforests of Queensland, Australia, a newly discovered spider has been found using a special method to catch its prey. This spider belongs to the genus Propostira and hunts green tree ants.

**What happened** – Scientists observed that this spider builds a cone-shaped snare trap that works like an ancient Roman weapon called a ballista. When a green tree ant bites the trap, the spider’s snare releases and flings the ant nearly 30 centimeters into the air, where it lands in the spider’s main web. This happens extremely fast, with forces strong enough to measure 130 times the pull of gravity.

**Impact** – This discovery is important because it shows a new way animals use tools and physics to catch food. The spider’s trap is very precise, only attracting green tree ants and using sticky silk to hold them tight. It also keeps the spider safe by lifting the ant away from the forest floor, where the ant’s colony might attack.

**What’s next step** – Researchers hope to learn more about how the spider’s silk and trap work. This could help scientists understand natural engineering better and inspire new ideas in technology, like making stronger or smarter materials.

**One-sentence takeaway** – A newly found Australian spider uses a clever trap that catapults ants into its web, revealing nature’s surprising use of physics and design.
articles.personality_title_fr: Une nouvelle araignée australienne utilise un piège unique pour lancer des fourmis dans sa toile
articles.personality_presentation_fr:
**Contexte** – Dans les forêts tropicales du Queensland, en Australie, une araignée récemment découverte utilise une méthode spéciale pour attraper ses proies. Cette araignée appartient au genre Propostira et chasse les fourmis vertes des arbres.

**Ce qui s’est passé** – Les scientifiques ont observé que cette araignée construit un piège en forme de cône qui fonctionne comme une arme romaine ancienne appelée baliste. Lorsqu’une fourmi verte mord le piège, le snare de l’araignée se libère et projette la fourmi à près de 30 centimètres dans les airs, où elle atterrit dans la toile principale de l’araignée. Cela se passe très rapidement, avec une force 130 fois supérieure à la gravité.

**Impact** – Cette découverte est importante car elle montre une nouvelle façon dont les animaux utilisent des outils et la physique pour attraper leur nourriture. Le piège de l’araignée est très précis, attirant uniquement les fourmis vertes et utilisant une soie collante pour les retenir. Cela protège aussi l’araignée en éloignant la fourmi du sol, où la colonie pourrait attaquer.

**Prochaine étape** – Les chercheurs espèrent mieux comprendre comment fonctionne la soie et le piège de l’araignée. Cela pourrait aider à mieux saisir l’ingénierie naturelle et inspirer de nouvelles idées technologiques, comme la création de matériaux plus solides ou intelligents.

**Résumé en une phrase** – Une araignée australienne récemment découverte utilise un piège ingénieux qui catapulte les fourmis dans sa toile, révélant l’usage surprenant de la physique et du design par la nature.
articles.personality_title_es: Nueva araña australiana usa una trampa única para lanzar hormigas a su telaraña
articles.personality_presentation_es:
**Contexto** – En las selvas tropicales de Queensland, Australia, se ha descubierto una araña que usa un método especial para atrapar a sus presas. Esta araña pertenece al género Propostira y caza hormigas verdes de los árboles.

**Qué pasó** – Los científicos observaron que esta araña construye una trampa en forma de cono que funciona como un arma romana antigua llamada balista. Cuando una hormiga verde muerde la trampa, la red de la araña se libera y lanza a la hormiga casi 30 centímetros en el aire, donde cae en la telaraña principal de la araña. Esto ocurre muy rápido, con fuerzas 130 veces mayores que la gravedad.

**Impacto** – Este descubrimiento es importante porque muestra una nueva forma en que los animales usan herramientas y física para atrapar comida. La trampa de la araña es muy precisa, atrayendo solo a las hormigas verdes y usando seda pegajosa para atraparlas. También protege a la araña al levantar a la hormiga del suelo, donde la colonia podría atacar.

**Próximo paso** – Los investigadores esperan aprender más sobre cómo funciona la seda y la trampa de la araña. Esto podría ayudar a entender mejor la ingeniería natural e inspirar nuevas ideas tecnológicas, como crear materiales más fuertes o inteligentes.

**Resumen en una frase** – Una araña australiana recién descubierta usa una trampa inteligente que catapulta hormigas a su telaraña, mostrando el sorprendente uso de la física y el diseño en la naturaleza.
articles.image_prompt: A detailed, warm-toned painting of a delicate conical spider web shaped like a Roman ballista, suspended beneath a lush rainforest leaf, with a vibrant green tree ant caught mid-air being gently flung from the snare trap into a higher, intricate spider web above.
